summary: The PoC demonstrates that shell command construction in SSH operations fails to escape shell metacharacters, allowing command injection via disk configuration parameters through command substitution $(), semicolons, and pipes.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should demonstrate that malicious file_id values with shell metacharacters ($(), ;, |) are executed as commands on the SSH target, proving command injection capability.
actual_observed_result: The simulated SSH execution showed that commands containing $(command), ;command, and |command patterns were interpreted by bash and would execute on the Proxmox node. The injection_detected.log was created confirming command injection.
verification_method: The run script simulates createCustomDisk command construction and mock SSH execution. It tests multiple injection patterns (command substitution, semicolons, pipes) and verifies by checking for injection detection logs.
evidence_produced_by_run: Mock SSH logs show injected commands like $(id) and wget being passed to bash execution
/var/log/injection_detected.log was created when shell metacharacters were found
Command construction analysis shows only single-quote escaping, no escaping of $, ;, |, etc.
Shell script demonstration proves bash interprets user input as commands

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the repository and builds a minimal test environment that simulates SSH command execution.
